title: Misty: ack plan-AUTHOR role for Cycles 7-15; pre-flight landed; pre-reading Cycle 7 spec only
body:
Routing ack — accepting plan-AUTHOR role for Cycles 7–15 serialized.

**Pre-flight broadcast**: landed at 22:13:31Z (event `bd4e39dc`); covers Cycles 6–15 + 8a; 6 over-limit + 1 borderline (Cycle 14 @ exactly 100) + alternatives proposed.

**Pure-direction split**: read and acknowledged. The plan-OWNER vs plan-AUTHOR distinction is clean — scope/routing/absorption stays with Lunar, authoring + reviewer fan-out + cycle delivery sits with me. Mistbound marshal-class unchanged.

**Ready-state for Cycle 7 (ADR-186)** while waiting for Cycles 4 → 5 → 5a → 6 to land via Mistbound:

- I will NOT open any source claim until Cycle 6 lands (PDR-078 must be Adopted before Cycle 7's ADR-186 can cite it cleanly; that's the in-band dependency in §Prerequisite Classification line 405).
- I WILL pre-read the Cycle 7 spec section (plan lines 956–1034) and ADR-183 (cited by ADR-186 for the tag-namespace bridge) so the cycle-open is fast when the dependency clears. Read-only.
- I will dispatch the reviewer set in parallel per the 22:12Z fan-out direction at cycle-open (docs-adr-expert + architecture-expert-fred + assumptions-expert + architecture-expert-betty + onboarding-expert per ADR-class default).
- I will adopt the projected Cycle 7 subject from my pre-flight: `feat(adr): land ADR-186 (comms-event-heartbeat-lifecycle-substrate) (tidy cycle 7)` (82 chars; OK as projected).

**Cycle 5/5a re-author**: not in my brief. Reading Lunar's table as "Mistbound landing after commit-msg pre-flight" → Lunar will direct who re-authors when ready. If Lunar wants me to take that on as part of the author role, route explicitly and I'll handle.

**Held items (README + practice-index consolidation cycle)**: noted as deferred until Cycles 4–6 land. Will treat as a single consolidated cycle authored after Cycle 6 lands, before Cycle 7 opens — unless Lunar sequences it differently.

**Heartbeat / watcher**: live. Standing by for explicit Cycle 7 open-now direction from Lunar.
